summary: Implementation satisfies all hard checklist items for a conformance-gate change. The spec's Test Plan requires porting CPython 3.12 test_generators.py subset; the implementation delivers exactly that as 6 new test files (1088 lines) under crates/cclab-mamba/tests/conformance/generators/ driven by a new conformance_generators.rs test binary. 36 #[test] functions total: 23 passing (R1-R5 core generator protocol) + 12 #[ignore] (R6 iterator protocol on user classes, R7 async generators) — ignored cases document pre-existing runtime gaps with the same root cause as existing generator_conformance_tests, explicitly scoped out of this change per post_clarifications. Per issue #756's design, this change is the conformance GATE that #1187's coroutine rewrite must pass unmodified (AC8) — landing the tests against the current channel-based runtime establishes the baseline. @spec annotations on every test function provide spec↔code traceability. No runtime source files modified, so no regression risk for existing tests.

checklist_results.requirement: R1 (P0) — core protocol: yield/next/StopIteration with StopIteration.value
checklist_results.details: test_generators_basic.rs exercises yield semantics, next() advance, StopIteration raising on exhaustion, and StopIteration.value from generator return. 6 tests, all passing.
test_generators_send_throw.rs covers g.send(value) happy path + first-send-must-be-None TypeError matching CPython message. Combined with R3 throw in same file (AC2, AC3). 6 tests, all passing.

checklist_results.requirement: R3 (P0) — throw(exc) exception injection with caught/uncaught paths
checklist_results.details: test_generators_send_throw.rs covers g.throw(ValueError) for both caught (continues iteration) and uncaught (propagates) paths. AC3. All passing.
test_generators_close.rs covers g.close() happy path + RuntimeError when generator catches GeneratorExit and yields again. 5 tests (AC4), all passing.

checklist_results.requirement: R5 (P0) — yield from delegation with send/throw/close forwarding
checklist_results.details: test_generators_yield_from.rs covers outer generator delegating to inner generator; send/throw/close propagate to the sub-iterator; StopIteration.value from sub-iterator becomes yield-from expression value. 6 tests (AC5), all passing.

checklist_results.requirement: R6 (P1) — iterator protocol __iter__/__next__ on user classes
checklist_results.details: test_iterator_protocol.rs contains 6 tests, all #[ignore]. The change spec and post_clarifications explicitly document that R6 is covered in tests only — fixing the user-class iterator gap is out of scope for this conformance-gate change. Pre-existing gap with same root cause as existing generator_conformance_tests entries.

checklist_results.requirement: R7 (P1) — async generators (async for, asend, athrow, aclose)
checklist_results.details: test_async_generators.rs contains 5 tests, all #[ignore]. The async generator driver has a pre-existing gap that's out of scope per post_clarifications. The tests are in place as a regression gate for when the async fix lands.

scenario_coverage.scenario: AC1 — def g(): yield 1; yield 2; return 3
AC2 — send happy path + first-send TypeError
AC4 — close + RuntimeError on ignored exit
AC6 — async for driving async generator
AC7 — CPython 3.12 commit hash header comment
AC8 — suite must survive #1187 perf rewrite unmodified
scenario_coverage.test: No runtime code modified; suite is the gate for the future rewrite.

soft_issues.message: 15 alignment-checker violations on the spec file (12 missing_section_annotation + 3 format_priority_violation on Wireframe/Component/Design Token stubs). These are already tracked as bug-spec-generator-does-not-emit-section-type-annotati.md — they are NOT implementation issues and should not block APPROVED. The annotations ARE present on every requested heading, just one line lower than the regex expects (architectural bug in replace_section).

The change spec's ## Changes section mentions `crates/cclab-mamba/src/runtime/async_generator.rs` as potentially needing a new file. The implementation did not create it (nothing to modify yet; tests for R7 are all #[ignore]). This is correctly deferred per post_clarifications but could be noted more explicitly in implementation.md.
